title: Araghchi Calls Muscat Talks 'A Good Start'
detail: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi described the Muscat talks as 'a good start,' with both sides agreeing to continue negotiations after consultations with their capitals. Iran's Fars News Agency reported the assessment was cautiously optimistic, though analysts noted that core differences on ballistic missiles and proxy support remained unresolved. A second round of talks was planned, with Geneva emerging as the likely venue.

title: Analysts Warn Muscat Talks 'Bought Time, Not a Deal'
detail: Regional security analysts assessed that the Muscat talks produced no breakthrough and may have merely delayed escalation rather than prevented it. Neither side signaled any shift from opening positions, with Iran insisting on nuclear-only discussions and the US demanding a comprehensive framework. The next few weeks would determine whether the talks laid foundations for progress or simply bought time before military escalation.

title: Oil Markets React to Uncertain Diplomacy
detail: Brent crude rose modestly following the inconclusive Muscat talks, as traders weighed the possibility of continued diplomacy against the backdrop of a massive US military buildup. Iranian crude was trading at an $11-12 per barrel discount below comparable benchmarks, up from $3 a year earlier, reflecting the bite of sanctions enforcement. Shipping insurers increased war risk premiums for Persian Gulf transits by 15%.
